title: Limiting argument from simple roots to general case is unjustified

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
After proving the Stam inequality for simple-root polynomials, the proof concludes:

  "The simple-root reduction (Lemma 2) extends this to all real-rooted p,q."

No argument is given. The intended route is: apply the proved inequality to (T_ε p, T_ε q), use (T_ε p)⊞_n(T_ε q) = T_ε²(p⊞_n q) (Lemma 2(iii)), then take ε→0 using Lemma 2(ii). However, the limiting step requires:

(a) 1/Φ_n(T_ε p) → 1/Φ_n(p) and likewise for q. This holds if Φ_n has the same limit behaviour at multiple roots (Φ_n→∞, so 1/Φ_n→0). The proof asserts this via Lemma 2(ii), but Lemma 2(ii) only states "Φ_n(T_ε p)→Φ_n(p)" as a bare claim justified by "continuity of Φ_n in the roots." Φ_n is NOT continuous at multiple roots (it is +∞ there); what is needed is that Φ_n is lower semicontinuous, so 1/Φ_n is upper semicontinuous. This is true but unproved.

(b) The inequality 1/Φ_n(T_ε²(p⊞_n q)) ≥ 1/Φ_n(T_ε p) + 1/Φ_n(T_ε q) holds for each ε>0 by the simple-root case. To conclude the ε=0 case one takes ε→0 and uses:
  - LHS: 1/Φ_n(T_ε²(p⊞_n q)) → 1/Φ_n(p⊞_n q) (requires USC of 1/Φ_n, i.e. LSC of Φ_n)
  - RHS: converges to 1/Φ_n(p)+1/Φ_n(q) (requires continuity / LSC argument).

The chain works but only after establishing LSC of Φ_n. LSC follows because as any two roots collide, the corresponding Cauchy-type sum in Φ_n diverges. However, a formal proof is needed, not merely asserted.

Specifically, Lemma 2(ii) should be restated as: Φ_n is lower semicontinuous in the roots (finite on simple-root polynomials, +∞ on polynomials with repeated roots), hence 1/Φ_n is upper semicontinuous. This is the property used in the limiting step.

comments.author: system
comments.body:
**Proof file:** `webapp/issues/q4/working_solution.tex`

The issue described above occurs in this working solution file. Reviewers should consult the file at that path for the exact context.
